title: pitcairnia neglecta
intro: Pitcairnia neglecta, also known as the 'Wild Pineapple', is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. The leaves of the Pitcairnia neglecta plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
